heading: Key Takeaway
text: Credit for the discovery of electron and proton goes to J.J.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: J.J. Thomson proposed that electrons are embedded in a positive sphere.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: STRUCTURE OF THE ATOM 45 Rutherford s alpha-particle scattering experiment led to the discovery of the atomic nucleus.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Rutherford s model of the atom proposed that a very tiny nucleus is present inside the atom and electrons revolve around this nucleus. The stability of the atom could not be explained by this model.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Neils Bohr s model of the atom was more successful. He proposed that electrons are distributed in different shells with discrete energy around the nucleus. If the atomic shells are complete, then the atom will be stable and less reactive.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: J. Chadwick discovered presence of neutrons in the nucleus of an atom. So, the three sub-atomic particles of an atom are: (i) electrons, (ii) protons and (iii) neutrons. Electrons are negatively charged, protons are positively charged and neutrons have no charges. The mass of an electron is about times the mass of an hydrogen atom. The mass of a proton and a neutron is taken as one unit each.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Shells of an atom are designated as K,L,M,N, .

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Valency is the combining capacity of an atom.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: The atomic number of an element is the same as the number of protons in the nucleus of its atom.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: The mass number of an atom is equal to the number of nucleons in its nucleus.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Isotopes are atoms of the same element, which have different mass numbers.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Isobars are atoms having the same mass number but different atomic numbers.

heading: Key Takeaway
text: Elements are defined by the number of protons they possess.
